run.__comment_batch: USER-SETTABLE. Each item is an independent background realization; averaging the gradient over the batch is what stabilizes training. Approx GPU peak (500 ms rollout, full BPTT): batch 8 ~5 GB, batch 16 ~8-9 GB, batch 32 ~15.7 GB (32 measured; 8/16 linear estimates). Larger batch = smoother training, more memory.

gamma.__comment_band: USER-EDITABLE gamma window (40 +/- 10 Hz). With frame_len=200 @ dt=1ms the PSD is evaluated on a 5 Hz grid (0,5,10,...), so 30/50 align exactly with bin edges. The frequency the network can be trained toward is set by the I->E delay (build_net.DELAY_IE) + inhibitory decay (build_net.BASIS_INH); faster inhibition / shorter delay -> higher frequency (retune the delay AND this band together).
gamma.__comment_ref: spectral_ref='full' compares in-band power to the WHOLE spectrum (penalizes power at all other frequencies -> a smoother, more monotonic training signal); 'flanks' compares only to the immediate shoulders above. 'flanks' entries are only used when spectral_ref='flanks' (and to locate the reported peak).
gamma.__comment_ngroups: n_groups=1 => AVERAGE-FIRST (spectrum of the population mean = genuine coordination). n_groups>1 averages sub-group spectra (rewards per-cell regularity too).
gamma.__comment_rate: Per-cell rates shaped by EMD toward a LOGNORMAL (median rate_median_hz, spread rate_sigma) => realistic sparse rates, curbs runaway >100 Hz cells and silent cells. rate_median_hz is USER-SETTABLE.
